title: sedum rosea var. vulgare
intro: Sedum rosea var. vulgare, also known as Woodland Stonecrop, is a perennial plant species belonging to the succulent family Crassulaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Sedum rosea var. vulgare include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although proper dosages and usage should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
